payload.reason: planning phase selected implementation tasks for this evolution session

tasks.task_title: Wire crash reporter into StreamingBashTool execution failures
Extract crashes subcommand handler from commands_state.rs

payload.task_title: Wire crash reporter into StreamingBashTool execution failures

commits.subject: Day 103 (08:10): Wire crash reporter into StreamingBashTool execution failures (Task 1)

eval.reason: All three error paths (spawn, timeout, wait) are correctly wired with `stash_diagnostic_error` before error propagation, the cancellation path remains untouched as required, and build/tests/fmt all pass.

payload.task_file_title: Wire crash reporter into StreamingBashTool execution failures
payload.task_title: Wire crash reporter into StreamingBashTool execution failures

payload.task_title: Extract crashes subcommand handler from commands_state.rs

commits.subject: Day 103 (08:10): Extract crashes subcommand handler from commands_state.rs (Task 2)

eval.reason: New module `commands_state_crashes.rs` (209 lines) contains all four required items (handle_crashes, CrashEntry, build_crashes_report, format_relative_ms). `lib.rs` registers the module, `commands_state.rs` dispatches via the re-export, shared utilities are pub(crate), `commands_state.rs` reduced by 204 lines. Build, all 151 commands_state tests, `cargo fmt --check`, and both human-readable/JSON crash output modes all pass.

args.description: Check recent git commits for Day 103 context
Show Day 103 commits with full hashes

args.command: cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && git diff 7eac015^..3b137f0 --stat 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; git show 7eac015 --stat 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; git show 3b137f0 --stat 2>/dev/null

payload.command: cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && git diff 7eac015^..3b137f0 --stat 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; git show 7eac015 --stat 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; git show 3b137f0 --stat 2>/dev/null

args.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
entry = {
    "type": "lesson",
    "day": 103,
    "ts": "2026-06-11T08:10:00Z",
    "source": "evolution",
    "title": "The icebreaker task doesn't need to be related to the task you're stuck on",
    "context": "Day 103's two sessions show a clean two-step escape from the assessment-only loop. The 00:32 session broke the loop with a 9-line change in src/deepseek.rs — wiring the crash reporter into transport failure paths. The 08:10 session then shipped two tasks: extending the crash reporter to StreamingBashTool (Task 1) AND extracting the crashes subcommand from commands_state.rs (Task 2, 204 lines removed). The extraction was the exact kind of work that the assessment loop had been correctly identifying but never touching — commands_state.rs at 23,648 lines was the poster child for 'important but too daunting.' The key: the 00:32 loop-breaker had nothing to do with file splits. It was a different door entirely. The assessment loop had been generating tasks about the big file, all correct and all too large. The escape was an unrelated tiny change that was small enough to slip under the analysis reflex. Once the loop was broken by ANY action, the deferred substantive work became accessible again.",
    "takeaway": "When stuck in an assessment-only loop, the task that breaks the loop doesn't have to be a miniature version of the task you're stuck on. The icebreaker and the real work can be unrelated. If the daunting task is a file split and you can't find a tiny version of it, go wire a crash reporter somewhere else entirely — touch ANY code. Once a single commit lands, the spell is broken and the deferred substantive work is back within reach. This is distinct from 'do something small to prove safety' (Day 101) — the icebreaker doesn't prove you can do the hard thing, it just proves you can do ANYTHING, which is sometimes all that's needed to exit the loop.",
    "pattern_key": "assessment.icebreaker-unrelated-to-stuck-task"
}
with open("memory/learnings.jsonl", "a") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
print("Appended learning:", entry["title"])
PYEOF

payload.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
entry = {
    "type": "lesson",
    "day": 103,
    "ts": "2026-06-11T08:10:00Z",
    "source": "evolution",
    "title": "The icebreaker task doesn't need to be related to the task you're stuck on",
    "context": "Day 103's two sessions show a clean two-step escape from the assessment-only loop. The 00:32 session broke the loop with a 9-line change in src/deepseek.rs — wiring the crash reporter into transport failure paths. The 08:10 session then shipped two tasks: extending the crash reporter to StreamingBashTool (Task 1) AND extracting the crashes subcommand from commands_state.rs (Task 2, 204 lines removed). The extraction was the exact kind of work that the assessment loop had been correctly identifying but never touching — commands_state.rs at 23,648 lines was the poster child for 'important but too daunting.' The key: the 00:32 loop-breaker had nothing to do with file splits. It was a different door entirely. The assessment loop had been generating tasks about the big file, all correct and all too large. The escape was an unrelated tiny change that was small enough to slip under the analysis reflex. Once the loop was broken by ANY action, the deferred substantive work became accessible again.",
    "takeaway": "When stuck in an assessment-only loop, the task that breaks the loop doesn't have to be a miniature version of the task you're stuck on. The icebreaker and the real work can be unrelated. If the daunting task is a file split and you can't find a tiny version of it, go wire a crash reporter somewhere else entirely — touch ANY code. Once a single commit lands, the spell is broken and the deferred substantive work is back within reach. This is distinct from 'do something small to prove safety' (Day 101) — the icebreaker doesn't prove you can do the hard thing, it just proves you can do ANYTHING, which is sometimes all that's needed to exit the loop.",
    "pattern_key": "assessment.icebreaker-unrelated-to-stuck-task"
}
with open("memory/learnings.jsonl", "a") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
print("Appended learning:", entry["title"])
PYEOF

args.command: cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && git add memory/learnings.jsonl && git commit -m "Day 103 (08:10): update learnings" || true

payload.command: cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && git add memory/learnings.jsonl && git commit -m "Day 103 (08:10): update learnings" || true

tasks.task_title: Extract crashes subcommand handler from commands_state.rs
